leads.name: Ali Kriegsman
leads.notes: Exited founder and consultant building a branded business book project 'The Raise' with a sophisticated DTC launch strategy. Prime candidate for a 500–1,000 copy offset print run designed for speaking engagements and direct sales. Cushing-Malloy's single point of contact and 20-day turnaround aligns directly with her high-standard launch demands.

leads.name: Allegra Hall
leads.notes: Indie paranormal romance author with 3 published titles in her Lost Moon Universe, most recently 'Howl' (Nov 2024). Active on Threads and Instagram with engaged reader community. Currently printing via POD — a strong candidate to upgrade to a 500–1,500 copy offset run for better quality and margins. Direct email confirmed on her carrd site.

leads.source: Indie Author / Amazon KDP Upgrade Candidate
leads.name: Rob Dircks
leads.notes: Self-published over 10,000 copies of his 'Where the Hell is Tesla?' series and now runs Goldfinch Publishing helping other authors produce books. Dual opportunity: his own upcoming titles plus potential referrals from Goldfinch author clients. Cushing-Malloy's white-glove guidance and volume pricing education would resonate strongly with his author-centric approach.

leads.name: Veronica Llorca-Smith
leads.notes: Substack Bestseller with 13K+ paid subscribers on 'The Lemon Tree Mindset.' Previously co-authored a collaborative book and is positioned for a solo self-published project. Cushing-Malloy's single point of contact and hand-holding for first-time independent projects would be a compelling differentiator.

leads.name: Maren Moore
leads.notes: USA Today bestselling indie romance author with multiple 2024 releases and a strong social following. Currently self-published on POD with an active direct-sales model. Cushing-Malloy's volume pricing education and cost-per-unit breakdown vs. POD would immediately demonstrate ROI.

leads.name: Oriana Leckert
leads.notes: Director of Publishing at Kickstarter with deep expertise in crowdfunded book economics. High-value connector who advises book creators on production decisions. Outreach framing Cushing-Malloy as the trusted offset printing partner for Kickstarter book campaigns could open significant referral flow.

leads.name: Crystal Wilkinson
leads.notes: Award-winning food memoirist and cookbook author of 'Praisesong for the Kitchen Ghosts' (2024). University of Kentucky professor exploring limited-edition or self-published follow-up print runs. Cushing-Malloy's premium offset quality and Ann Arbor heritage would resonate with her craft-focused audience.

leads.name: Claire Kingsley
leads.notes: Amazon #1 bestselling indie romance author with 46+ published books and a large established reader base. At her volume, switching even a portion of her catalog to offset printing would meaningfully improve per-unit cost. Cushing-Malloy's volume pricing education session would be high value.

leads.name: Cate C. Wells
leads.notes: Indie paranormal romance author with 22K+ social media followers and multiple 2024 titles. Solid candidate for a 500–1,000 copy offset print run upgrade from POD. Cushing-Malloy's single point of contact and 20-day turnaround would reassure a creator managing production alongside content creation.

leads.name: Cornerstone Press (UW-Stevens Point)
leads.notes: University-affiliated literary small press at UW-Stevens Point, Wisconsin, publishing regional fiction, poetry, and nonfiction. Great Lakes geography and active acquisition pipeline makes this a natural Cushing-Malloy fit. Pitch the consistent 20-day production timeline and single manufacturing contact as ideal for their academic publishing calendar.

leads.name: Restless Books
leads.notes: Independent publisher specializing in international literature in translation with an active Bookshop.org storefront and 10–15 titles per year. Cushing-Malloy's reliability, single point of contact, and offset quality credentials are a strong fit for their literary list.

leads.name: Wandering in the Words Press
leads.notes: IBPA member micro press specializing in speculative fiction with 3–5 titles per year and actively acquiring for 2026. Cushing-Malloy's competitive 250-copy starting point and print run economics education would speak directly to a micro press managing tight production budgets.

leads.name: Talia Fox
leads.notes: CEO and leadership strategist with a published business authority book 'The Power of Choice' and an active Fortune 500 speaking calendar. Books are a core business development tool — she gifts them at keynotes. Cushing-Malloy's 20-day turnaround and volume pricing breakdown for 500 vs. 1,000 copies would be highly persuasive.
